steps.title: Identify the contrasting outcome
steps.content:
The passage first describes one result: “Many of the regions' islands and groups of islands have become independent nations.” Then it introduces the Northern Mariana Islands and Society Islands as the opposite—they're part of the US and French Polynesia, NOT independent.

This is the other side of the “range of outcomes” mentioned at the start.

steps.title: Match the transition
steps.content: ”On the other hand” marks the shift from independence to dependence—from one outcome to its direct opposite. The other choices fail to signal this contrast between the two competing outcomes. That matches choice D — D is correct.
